blocks.body: Articles: Common Mistakes shows how not to confuse a / an / the, when to leave the zero article, and how to separate general meaning from specific reference.

{"level":"A2-B1","intro":"Mistakes with articles usually appear when we have not decided whether we mean one nonspecific thing, a known specific thing, or an idea in general.","rules":[{"label":"A / AN","color":"emerald","text":"Use a / an with a singular countable noun when you mention something for the first time or do not specify which one you mean.","example":"She is a teacher. / I need an umbrella."},{"label":"THE","color":"blue","text":"Use the when the listener already knows the thing or when the noun becomes specific because of the context.","example":"The file on your desk is ready."},{"label":"ZERO ARTICLE","color":"amber","text":"We often use no article for plural nouns and uncountable nouns in general, and for some institutions in their main function.","example":"People need sleep. / I go to school every day."}]}

blocks.heading: 1. Where Learners Most Often Get Lost
blocks.body:
With articles, the real problem is often not the article itself but the type of meaning behind the noun.

  • A / an fits one example from a group: a book, an idea, a teacher.
  • The fits something already known from the situation, an earlier mention, or a defining phrase: the book on the table.
  • Zero article is common when we talk about something in general: Books are useful. Coffee helps me focus.
  • A separate trap is institutions: go to school for the activity, but go to the school for the building.

blocks.heading: 2. How the Correct Choice Works
blocks.body:
Ask yourself three quick questions.

  1. Is this one item or a general idea? If it is one example from a group, you often need a / an.
  2. Does the listener already know which one? If yes, you often need the.
  3. Am I talking about a plural or uncountable noun in general? Then the zero article often works.

Compare: I bought a laptop yesterday. -> first mention. The laptop is already set up. -> now it is specific. Laptops are expensive. -> general statement.

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Leaving out the article before a profession or another singular countable noun.","wrong":"She is teacher.","right":"She is a teacher.","hint":"After be, a profession or role in the singular usually needs a / an."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Adding the when an institution means its main function.","wrong":"I went to the school every day when I was a child.","right":"I went to school every day when I was a child.","hint":"When school, university, bed, church, or prison mean their usual function, the article often disappears."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Using the before a plural noun with general meaning.","wrong":"The people need better sleep.","right":"People need better sleep.","hint":"For general statements about people, books, or cars, we usually use the zero article."},{"label":"Mistake 4","color":"rose","title":"Using an article before an uncountable noun in a general sense.","wrong":"I love the coffee, but I do not drink it at night.","right":"I love coffee, but I do not drink it at night.","hint":"If you mean coffee in general, no article is needed. The coffee means a specific coffee."}]}

{"title":"4. Quick takeaways","items":["Check whether the noun is a singular countable noun. If it is, it often sounds incomplete without a determiner.","Use the when the noun is already defined by context or a clear detail.","For plural nouns and uncountable nouns in general meaning, the zero article is often the right choice.","Study institution phrases separately, such as go to school, be at home, and in hospital where that variety of English uses them."]}
